source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Hoarseness (Dysphonia) (Update) – official fact sheet and key action statements
source.population: Patients of all ages with dysphonia or hoarseness.
source.exclusions: Exact for dysphonia assessment and escalation; referral pathways require UAE clinician review.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Defines dysphonia and hoarseness, describes voice-quality changes, common causes, vocal demand, tobacco exposure, reflux context, and prevention topics.

source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Hoarseness (Dysphonia) (Update) – official fact sheet and key action statements
source.population: Patients of all ages with dysphonia or hoarseness.
source.exclusions: Exact for dysphonia assessment and escalation; referral pathways require UAE clinician review.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Defines dysphonia and hoarseness, describes voice-quality changes, common causes, vocal demand, tobacco exposure, reflux context, and prevention topics.

items.rationale: The exact dysphonia history section supports onset, duration, impact, associated symptoms, relevant negatives, smoking or professional-voice context, and clinician-performed ENT or neck assessment without diagnosing the cause.

items.final_wording: Supports clinician recognition of recommendation-qualified persistent unexplained hoarseness, unexplained or persistent neck lump, oral ulceration lasting more than 3 weeks, lip or oral-cavity lump, red or red-and-white oral patch, and unexplained thyroid lump. It does not assert cancer or generate referral.

source.title: Suspected cancer: recognition and referral
source.population: Children, young people, and adults with symptoms or signs that may indicate cancer, subject to recommendation-specific age criteria.
source.setting: Primary care recognition, safety netting, investigation, and referral.
source.exclusions: Exact for selected suspicious skin-lesion, safety-netting, and age- or risk-qualified respiratory recommendations; international suspected-cancer pathways require UAE clinician review. This batch also reviews recommendation-qualified upper-GI dysphagia, jaundice, and colorectal rectal-bleeding fields; no cancer diagnosis or automatic referral text is generated. exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.8.1–1.8.5 — laryngeal, oral, and thyroid cancer recognition features

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician recognition of recommendation-qualified persistent unexplained hoarseness, unexplained or persistent neck lump, oral ulceration lasting more than 3 weeks, lip or oral-cavity lump, red or red-and-white oral patch, and unexplained thyroid lump. It does not assert cancer or generate referral.

items.rationale: The exact NICE head-and-neck section supports clinician concern when persistent unexplained hoarseness or an unexplained neck lump is documented in recommendation-qualified age context; it does not assert cancer or generate referral.

items.final_wording: Supports clinician-documented preventive discussion, questions, follow-up, and change in voice or quality of life.

source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Hoarseness (Dysphonia) (Update) – official fact sheet and key action statements
source.population: Patients of all ages with dysphonia or hoarseness.
source.exclusions: Exact for dysphonia assessment and escalation; referral pathways require UAE clinician review.

exact_location.heading: Key action statements: education and outcomes

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician-documented preventive discussion, questions, follow-up, and change in voice or quality of life.

items.rationale: The exact education and outcomes section supports concerns, clinician-entered plan, safety net, follow-up, and questions without recommending laryngoscopy, imaging, medicine, voice therapy, or referral.

items.final_wording: Requires history and examination for modifying factors and expedited laryngeal evaluation for neck mass, respiratory distress or stridor, tobacco history, recent procedures, and persistent symptoms.

source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Hoarseness (Dysphonia) (Update) – official fact sheet and key action statements
source.population: Patients of all ages with dysphonia or hoarseness.
source.exclusions: Exact for dysphonia assessment and escalation; referral pathways require UAE clinician review.

exact_location.heading: Key action statements: assessment, escalation, and laryngoscopy

source.evidence_paraphrase: Requires history and examination for modifying factors and expedited laryngeal evaluation for neck mass, respiratory distress or stridor, tobacco history, recent procedures, and persistent symptoms.

items.rationale: The exact escalation and laryngoscopy section was reviewed for red-flag context, but no automatic examination, imaging, or referral action was mapped.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.8.1–1.8.5 — laryngeal, oral, and thyroid cancer recognition features

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician recognition of recommendation-qualified persistent unexplained hoarseness, unexplained or persistent neck lump, oral ulceration lasting more than 3 weeks, lip or oral-cavity lump, red or red-and-white oral patch, and unexplained thyroid lump. It does not assert cancer or generate referral.

added_items.rationale: The exact NICE head-and-neck section supports clinician concern when persistent unexplained hoarseness or an unexplained neck lump is documented in recommendation-qualified age context; it does not assert cancer or generate referral.

added_items.final_wording: Supports clinician-documented preventive discussion, questions, follow-up, and change in voice or quality of life.

source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Hoarseness (Dysphonia) (Update) – official fact sheet and key action statements
source.population: Patients of all ages with dysphonia or hoarseness.
source.exclusions: Exact for dysphonia assessment and escalation; referral pathways require UAE clinician review.

exact_location.heading: Key action statements: education and outcomes

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician-documented preventive discussion, questions, follow-up, and change in voice or quality of life.

added_items.rationale: The exact education and outcomes section supports concerns, clinician-entered plan, safety net, follow-up, and questions without recommending laryngoscopy, imaging, medicine, voice therapy, or referral.

added_items.final_wording: Requires history and examination for modifying factors and expedited laryngeal evaluation for neck mass, respiratory distress or stridor, tobacco history, recent procedures, and persistent symptoms.

added_items.rationale: The exact escalation and laryngoscopy section was reviewed for red-flag context, but no automatic examination, imaging, or referral action was mapped.
